REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH MILES' MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. THIS ACTION HAS BEEN DISMISSED WITHOUT OBTAINING A COURT ORDER RETAINING JURISDICTION. IT IS NOT ENOUGH FOR PARTIES TO STIPULATE UNDER CCP 664.6, THE COURT MUST RETAIN JURISDICTION. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE PROVIDED BY THE MOVING PARTY THAT THAT PARTIES REQUESTED THAT THE COURT RETAIN JURISDICTION AND THAT THE COURT DID IN FACT RETAIN JURISDICTION. ADDITIONALLY, EVEN IF THE COURT HAD JURISDICTION THE MOTION WOULD BE DENIED. THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT SPECIFIC ENOUGH FOR ENFORCEMENT. AT THE TIME THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO THE PARTIES WERE ON NOTICE THAT THEY HAD A DIFFERENCE OF OPINIONS AS THE NUMBER OF WOODEN BOXES IN DEFENDANT'S POSSESSION - PLAINTIFF CLAIMED THAT AS OF 2009 (THE DATE OF LAST INVOICE) HE HAD 159 BOXES AND DEFENDANT ESTIMATED THAT THERE WERE 50 BOXES IN THE GARAGE. THE PARTIES FAILED TO RESOLVE THIS INCONSISTENCY AND ENTERED INTO THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WHEREAS DEFENDANTS WERE TO TURN OVER BOXES IN THEIR POSSESSION. SETTLEMENT DOES NOT PROVIDE A NUMBER AND THE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVIDE COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE AS TO THE NUMBER OF BOXES IN THE GARAGE IN 2011 (AT THE TIME PLAINTIFF LOST POSSESSION). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOSEPH MILES' Motion To Enforce Settlement is DENIED. This action has been dismissed without obtaining a Court order retaining jurisdiction. It is not enough for parties to stipulate under CCP 664.6, the Court must retain jurisdiction. There is no evidence provided by the moving party that that parties requested that the Court retain jurisdiction and that the Court did in fact retain jurisdiction. Additionally, even if the Court had jurisdiction the motion would be DENIED. The settlement agreement is not specific enough for enforcement. At the time the settlement agreement was entered into the parties were on notice that they had a difference of opinions as the number of wooden boxes in Defendant's possession - Plaintiff claimed that as of 2009 (the date of last invoice) he had 159 boxes and Defendant estimated that there were 50 boxes in the garage. The parties failed to resolve this inconsistency and entered into the settlement agreement whereas Defendants were to turn over boxes in their possession. Settlement does not provide a number and the Plaintiff fails to provide competent admissible evidence as to the number of boxes in the garage in 2011 (at the time Plaintiff lost possession). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)
